recommended_pair.reason: Best accuracy/click balance: concrete $42k car + Supreme Court line, without implying Timbs himself was never charged.

title_candidates.title: Police Took His $42,000 Car. The Supreme Court Drew a Line.
title_candidates.reason: Specific, accurate, emotional, and clear. It does not overclaim that Timbs was uncharged.

title_candidates.title: Can Police Take Your Car Without Convicting You?
title_candidates.reason: Strong broad question and high curiosity, but the Timbs facts include a guilty plea, so description must clarify the system-level point.

title_candidates.title: The Supreme Court Case That Put a Limit on Civil Forfeiture
title_candidates.reason: Very accurate and searchable, but less emotionally immediate.

title_candidates.title: The Government Seized His Land Rover. A 700-Year-Old Rule Stopped It.
title_candidates.reason: Distinctive and story-driven. Slightly long, but the Magna Carta angle is memorable.

title_candidates.title: When the Government Accuses Your Property Instead of You
title_candidates.reason: Conceptually strong, but less clickable and less tied to the Supreme Court payoff.
